verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 subunit beta) is canonically the catalytic beta subunit of the mitochondrial F1Fo-ATP synthase, residing in the mitochondrial inner membrane matrix-facing F1 head. However, there is well-documented evidence that this protein translocates to the plasma membrane outer leaflet under non-baseline conditions: (1) tumor/cancer cells display ectopic cell-surface ATP synthase complexes including ATP5F1B on the outer leaflet, where it has been targeted by antibodies and natural ligands (angiostatin binds ecto-ATP synthase on endothelial/cancer cells); (2) cell-death/apoptotic states expose mitochondrial proteins on the outer leaflet; (3) it is detected by cell-surface proteomics on multiple cancer cell lines. The surface form is the target of angiostatin and various anti-tumor antibody approaches. This fits cell_state_induced (cancer/oncogenic transformation ecto-display). No documented stable surface display under baseline non-cancer conditions.
